Type check passes cleanly. Here's what was created:

## Session Router — `/projects/:absolute_path/sessions/`

### [model.ts](server/lib/api/versions/v1/routes/claude/projects/sessions/model.ts)
Added two new namespaces alongside the existing ones:
- **`ProjectSessionModel.DeleteSession`** — `Params` with `session_id`
- **`ProjectSessionModel.RenameSession`** — `Params` with `session_id` + `Body` with `title` (1–255 chars)

### [index.ts](server/lib/api/versions/v1/routes/claude/projects/sessions/index.ts)
Four endpoints, all mounted under the project middleware that already populates `c.get('project')` with sessions:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET /` | List all sessions for the project | Returns `project.sessions` array from the middleware context |
| `GET /:session_id` | Get a specific session | Finds by `session_id` in the project's sessions array |
| `DELETE /:session_id` | Delete a session | Calls `sdk.deleteSession()` after verifying the session exists |
| `PATCH /:session_id` | Rename a session | Calls `sdk.renameSession()` with the new title |

All endpoints use the existing `DOCS_TAGS.CLAUDE_SESSIONS` tag, follow the same `APIRouteSpec.authenticated()` + `validator()` pattern as the parent routes, and the router is already mounted in [projects/index.ts:165](server/lib/api/versions/v1/routes/claude/projects/index.ts#L165) via `router.route('/:absolute_path', sessionsRouter)`.
